# map-search

map-search is a Japanese local map search service. It uses map technology inspired by practical tips about free map APIs, Leaflet, OpenStreetMap, Google Places API, store information, reviews, and map-based application development.

## What The Service Does

- Lets users search nearby stores and facilities on a map.
- Filters places by category such as cafe, workspace, beauty, clinic, and sightseeing.
- Shows place cards with rating, area, price range, tags, offers, and calls to action.
- Sends users to reservation, official search, and Google Maps direction links.
- Provides a partner listing model for stores and facilities.

## Main Page

- `/`: Interactive local map search page with facility list, filters, keyword search, CTA links, and partner listing information.
- `/shizuoka/`: Shizuoka area entry page for city and genre based map search.
- `/aichi/`: Aichi area entry page for city and genre based map search.

## Intended Users

- People looking for nearby cafes, workspaces, salons, clinics, or sightseeing spots.
- Store owners who want more visits, bookings, calls, and map-based discovery.
- Site owners who want to monetize local search through listings, coupons, reservations, and MEO support.

## Technical Base

- Leaflet
- OpenStreetMap tiles
- Static sample place data
- Replaceable data layer for Google Places API, official store data, or self-submitted listings

## Cash Points

- Featured listings
- Coupon placement
- Reservation links
- Store page upgrades
- MEO and Google Business Profile support
- Review management support
- Store map implementation consulting
